core_model.structure_summary: A short-time asymptotic expansion of a derived quantity in terms of the cumulants of a random variable's distribution, yielding a polynomial-in-argument expression whose coefficients are set by the third and fourth cumulants scaled by powers of a small parameter.
core_model.canonical_form: q_\theta(x) = \phi(x)\big\{1 + \kappa_3 H_3(x)\,\theta^{\beta_1} + \kappa_4 H_4(x)\,\theta^{\beta_2} + \dots\big\}, \quad \sigma(z,\theta) = \kappa_2\big(1 + \kappa_3 c_1(z)\theta^{\beta_1} + \kappa_4 c_2(z)\theta^{\beta_2}\big) + o(\cdot)

variables.meaning: log of the underlying random variable / driven process
small time-to-maturity parameter driving the asymptotics
scaling exponents controlling the rate of vanishing terms
Brownian driver of the underlying stochastic-volatility process

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A stochastic differential equation for the log-price with a variance process, dZ = -1/2 v dt + sqrt(v) dW, whose short-time distributional moments generate the expansion.

notes: The paper is deliberately model-independent / distribution-based: the central object is a cumulant/Edgeworth-type asymptotic expansion (Hermite polynomials H_n, cumulants kappa_3, kappa_4) of the return distribution and the resulting implied-volatility expansion, best tagged statistical_model. The dressing includes an underlying stochastic-volatility SDE dZ=-1/2 v dt + sqrt(v)dB, which is the natural process skeleton. Extraction is noisy and the model straddles statistical_model / algebraic asymptotics; moderate confidence.
